senses.definition: plural of bertillonage; the system or process of measuring and describing a person's physical characteristics for identification, historically used in criminal investigations
examples: The police kept records of bertillonages to help identify suspects.
Bertillonages were largely replaced by fingerprinting in modern forensic science.

senses.definition: surname of French origin; may refer to a person named Bertin
examples: Bertin published a theory in the 19th century.
We met a man named Bertin at the conference.

senses.definition: likely a proper noun or misspelling; not widely recognized in contemporary English; possibly a place, brand, or cultivar name
examples: The vase is labeled Bertolonia, but its origin is unclear.

senses.definition: a male given name, or a species of fish in some contexts; also an archaic or literary form of the name Bertram
examples: Bertram was the name of the knight in the tale.
The fisherman caught a bertram and released it.

senses.definition: a male given name of French origin; also used in surnames
examples: Bertrand was his grandfather's name.
The Bertrand model is used in statistics (note: separate usage).

senses.definition: a rare beryllium silicate mineral with formula Be4SiO4(SiO4)3; a source of beryllium
examples: Bertrandite is mined for beryllium production.
The mineral Bertrandite forms rounded crystals in pegmatites.

senses.definition: likely a misspelling or rare term; not recognized as a standard English word
examples: The label showed 'bertrum', which may be a typographical error.

senses.definition: covered or marked with a coarse, rounded bump or protuberance; puffed up with air or swelling
examples: The beruffed surface made the sculpture look ancient.
Her beruffed cheeks suggested fatigue.

senses.definition: ruffled or disheveled in appearance; made untidy or stirred up
examples: Her beruffled hair indicated a windy day.
The beruffled pages scattered as the book fell.

senses.definition: to run quickly or to proceed swiftly (archaic or dialectal usage)
examples: We berun to the gate to escape the rain.

senses.definition: to rustle; to make a soft, fluttering sound, often used in poetry
examples: The leaves berust softly in the breeze.

senses.definition: a proper noun; likely a surname or place name; not a common English word
examples: Bervie is the name of a small town in the region.

senses.definition: a town in England; also used in proper names; can refer to the Berwick measure (historical unit) or the border town Berwick-upon-Tweed
examples: Berwick-upon-Tweed lies near the border.

senses.definition: a member of the order Beryciformes, a group of ray-finned fish
examples: A berycid was caught in the aquarium's net.

senses.definition: relating to the Beryciformes or to berycid fish; bar-shaped or shield-like in some contexts
examples: Berycoid fins are distinctive in these species.

senses.definition: a taxonomic grouping within the superfamily Berycoidea, comprising certain berycid fishes
examples: The specimen belongs to the family within Berycoidea.

senses.definition: a member of the suborder Berycoidea or a similar grouping; related to berycoidean fishes
examples: The cichlid is not a berycoidean.

senses.definition: a taxonomic grouping, plural of berycoidea or related grouping within Beryciformes
examples: The two genera belong to berycoidei.

senses.definition: an order or grouping within Beryciformes; a taxonomic group of fishes
examples: Berycomorphi includes several deep-sea species.

senses.definition: a mineral that is often green or blue and used as a gemstone; beryl is the mineral family that includes emerald and aquamarine
examples: Emerald is a emerald variety of beryl.
The geologist noted a deep green beryl specimen.

senses.definition: to convert into or compound with beryllium; to form beryllate minerals or compounds
examples: The mineral can berlyate under high temperature.
Beryllate ions were identified in the sample.

senses.definition: a hypothetical or archaic term; not commonly used in modern English
examples: The term ber yline appeared in the old manuscript.
